Slayer reward point
This article has a calculator here.
Calculators determine experience and costs based on real-time prices from the Grand Exchange Market Watch.
Slayer points
Release date 5 June 2008 (Update)
Members No
Tradeable No
Approx. value
  • If buying runes for Slayer dart: 1,364 coins
  • If buying broad-tipped bolts: 400 coins
Cap 64,000
Examine Can be exchanged for rewards at any slayer master.
[view] • [talk]

Slayer points are a currency obtained by completing Slayer assignments or a special challenge from any Slayer Master except for Turael and Spria. The number rewarded depends on the Slayer Master, with the tougher masters awarding more points. Completion of the quest Smoking Kills will double the value of the points received. Additionally, points may be earned by trading in completed Tuska masks.

The maximum number of Slayer points a player can have at once is 64,000. However, this is not a hard cap. If a player completes a task with 63,999 points, then they will receive the entirety of the points from that task. Furthermore, Tuska masks can still be traded in for 20 points each after 64,000 points have been obtained.

When received from Raptor's chest of slaying, a different icon is used in the interface.

Slayer points can be earned in free-to-play by completing tasks from Jacquelyn, but they cannot be spent as a free player.

Obtaining points

[edit | edit source]

There are several ways of obtaining Slayer points: completing Slayer assignments, Slayer challenges, Soul reaper assignments, completing contracts on the Arc assigned by Ling, handing in completed Tuska masks, and opening the Raptor chest.

For an exhaustive list of all known sources for this item, see here.
SourceLevelQuantityRarityLeague region
Raptor's chest of slayingN/A 375AlwaysN/A

Assignments

[edit | edit source]

The points listed below are the full points per task, for which completion of the quest Smoking Kills is required. Before then, players will only receive half the listed points, rounded down (except for Jacqueline's regular tasks, which still award 1 point). If Smoking Kills has been completed, players will gain full points even if their membership expires.

A minimum of five tasks must be completed before points are awarded. Every tenth task awards five times the value of the rewarded points, with every 50th awarding fifteen times the value of the points. Completion of the entire Karamja achievements boosts the amount from Duradel/Lapalok's tenth tasks, but by a very minor amount.

The "Task streak" counter in the Slayer overlay counts the number of finished tasks. So, for example, if the "Task streak" says 49 (or 99, or 149, ...), finishing the current task will trigger the 15x point reward for the 50th task.

Point values after Smoking Kills
Slayer Master Points per task Points per 10th task Points per 50th task Notes
Turael or Spria 0 0 0 These Slayer masters will never award Slayer points and assignments will not contribute to completion streak. Cancelling a task to receive an easier one will reset the player's current completion streak to 0; However, completion streak will remain the same after accepting and completing a new task from these masters, as long as the previous task was completed and not cancelled.
Jacquelyn 1 5 15 Jacquelyn's tasks are easy, but also contribute to completion streak.
Vannaka 2 10 30 Vannaka's tasks are easy and fast, and can be used to complete the first four tasks and quickly begin earning points.
The Raptor ?–21 ?–105
(Multiples of 5) 		?–315
(Multiples of 15) 		The amount of Slayer points received from the Raptor's assignments depends on your Slayer level and the number of Slayer Trophies mounted. Each mounted trophy (up to a maximum of 3), as well as reaching level 113 (the halfway mark) and then level 120, increases the points received per task by one, up to 21 points at level 120 with all 3 trophies mounted.
Mazchna or Achtryn 4 20 60 Mazchna or Achtryn gives more experience per task than Vannaka.
Chaeldar 10 50 150 Chaeldar provides better experience and more Slayer points than Vannaka, despite taking longer to complete, as it can accumulate to more experience and points over a period of time. She is the highest level Slayer master to not have a Slayer level requirement.
Sumona 12 60 180 Sumona's tasks are very similar to Duradel's, though usually in lower quantities of monsters set. Sumona is best used by players who lack the combat and/or Slayer level to use Duradel. At high levels she can assign corrupted creatures, making her more profitable than using Jacquelyn as a buffer between 10th and 50th tasks.
Duradel or Lapalok 15 75
(80 after Elite Karamja achievements) 		225 These masters are recommended for point-earning tasks, due to the comparatively high number of points received upon completing their tasks.
Kuradal 18 90 270 Like Duradel or Lapalok, she is recommended for point-earning tasks, due to the comparatively high number of points received upon completing them. She requires 75 Slayer to use and should be used if the player cannot access Morvran or Laniakea.
Morvran 20 100 300 Like his predecessors listed above, he is recommended for point-earning tasks, due to the high number of points received from them. You should always use Morvran for point-earning tasks with 85 Slayer and completion of Plague's End unless you've unlocked Laniakea or want to risk wealth in the Wilderness.
Laniakea 22 110 330 Laniakea can assign "cluster tasks", which are groups of similar monsters designed to give the player freedom in which kind of monster they kill. A dragon cluster task, for example, allows the player to kill any kind of dragon instead of the regularly assigned black dragon etc. tasks.
Mandrith 22 110 330 He is also recommended for quick point-earning tasks, due to the high number of points received and the possibility of small and quick tasks. However, all of his tasks have to be done in the Wilderness. Therefore, caution is advised. Additionally, he awards a wilderness slayer chest on completion of each task. A single reaper point is also rewarded if the task is completed without dying or skipping.

Quick farming

[edit | edit source]

The best method to farm slayer points is to use Jacquelyn and block cave slimes, cave bugs, cows, frogs, ghosts and birds (requiring 100 Slayer points each and 300 quest points to unlock the needed slots). This will only result in her assigning spiders, goblins, skeletons, bats, rats, and zombies, all of whom can be found in the Lumbridge Catacombs. Use NPC Contact to reach Jacquelyn after completing each task from her. After completing the ninth task, head to a higher-level slayer master and complete the tenth or fiftieth task there if the player can or is willing to do it; a skip can be beneficial for particularly annoying tasks such as lava strykewyrms. The Slayer Introspection relic power is highly recommended to further speed up all tasks. The method's only drawback is the very steep requirements needed for optimal point gain. The method is further enhanced during events where slayer points are doubled.

Challenges

[edit | edit source]
Main article: Slayer/Challenges

Slayer challenges are sometimes given to players by Slayer Masters. They award the normal number of Slayer points (the number that would have been given if it was a task) plus bonus Slayer points and Slayer experience.

Slayer Master Additional Slayer points awarded
Turael or Spria 0
Jacquelyn 5
Vannaka 14
The Raptor 20[note 1]
Mazchna or Achtryn 3
Chaeldar 25
Sumona 30
Duradel or Lapalok 35
Kuradal 0[note 2]
Morvran 45
Mandrith 35
Laniakea 45
  1. ^ Does not count as a normal task, and regular points are not rewarded.
  2. ^ Although Kuradal doesn't give bonus slayer points, the challenge will still count as a normal task, and you will be rewarded accordingly.

Contracts

[edit | edit source]

Up to 8 Slayer points may be obtained per contract assigned by Ling that is completed on the Arc.

Tuska masks

[edit | edit source]

Tuska masks, obtained by slaying airut (requiring 92 Slayer and a task to get its parts) can be handed into any Slayer Master for 20 Slayer points per mask.

Raptor Chest

[edit | edit source]

The Raptor key, created by combining four key parts individually dropped by four different level 96 Slayer monsters (Wyverns for part 1, Ripper Demons for part 2, Camel Warriors for part 3, and Acheron mammoths for part 4), can be used to open the Raptor's chest of slaying in War's Retreat. The player gets 375 slayer points each time they open the chest, along with other rewards.

Rewards

[edit | edit source]
Main articles: Slayer Rewards and Death's Store

There are a variety of rewards which can be purchased using Slayer points, including learning how to craft Slayer equipment and gaining various Slayer-related abilities.

Having unlocked all abilities to craft Slayer items and the ability to deliver killing blows quicker is a requirement needed to get a trimmed completionist cape.

Once the helm upgrades have been purchased, any subsequent purchases of the upgrades are reduced to 10% of the original cost.

Slayer points can also be used to purchase certain rewards from Death's Store.

Learn

[edit source]

After buying the option to be assigned this monster, players can always go back to any slayer master to toggle this task off through the rewards interface, free of charge. Turning the task back on will require re-payment.

ItemNumber
in stock		Price
sold at		In-game descriptionNotes
Persuade Kuradal and Morvran to assign aquanites Unique50Ask Kuradal, Morvran or Laniakea to add Aquanites to their assignment list (with 78 Slayer).The player must have the required Slayer level, boosts will not work. Sumona and Duradel (or Lapalok) can still assign aquanites even with this toggled off.
Persuade Slayer masters to assign muspah Unique50Ask Kuradal, Morvran or Laniakea to add Muspah to their assignment list (with 76 Slayer. Requires Fate of the Gods).The player must have the required Slayer level, boosts will not work.
Persuade Slayer masters to assign nihil Unique50Ask Kuradal, Morvran or Laniakea to add Nihil to their assignment list (with 76 Slayer. Requires Fate of the Gods).The player must have the required Slayer level, boosts will not work.
Persuade Slayer masters to assign glacors Unique50Ask Kuradal, Morvran or Laniakea to add Glacor to their assignment list (requires Ritual of the Mahjarrat).
Persuade Slayer masters to assign tormented demons Unique50Ask Kuradal, Morvran or Laniakea to add Tormented Demons to their assignment list (requires While Guthix Sleeps).
Persuade Slayer masters to assign nightmare creatures Unique50Ask Kuradal, Morvran or Laniakea to add Nightmare creatures to their assignment list (requires Children of Mah).
Learn how to fletch broad arrows/bolts Unique300Learn how to fletch broad arrows (with 52 Fletching) and bolts (with 55 Fletching).
Learn how to craft rings of slaying Unique300Learn how to craft rings of slaying (with 75 Crafting).The Assist System can be used.
Learn how to deliver killing blows quicker Unique400Learn how to deliver killing blows quicker (with 75 Slayer).Monsters which require a finishing blow to kill, such as rock slugs, desert lizards, zygomites, and gargoyles will die when they reach zero life points like normal monsters, as long as the item required to kill them is in the backpack or tool belt. This ability functions with ranged and magic attacks; the player does not need to be positioned adjacent to the monster to activate the finishing blow effect.

This ability does not work when using experimental fungicide during Chaeldar's challenge.

The item may still be used to kill a monster slightly above zero life points as before.

It is stated in-game that 75 Slayer is required for this ability, but neither purchasing it nor using it actually requires the stated level.
Learn how to craft Slayer helmets Unique400Learn how to combine the nosepeg, earmuffs, spiny helm, facemask and uncharged black mask (with 55 Crafting)Smoking Kills is required to craft slayer helmets.
Learn how to fuse rings to full Slayer helmets Unique500Learn how to fuse Rings of Slaying and Ferocious Rings to your Full Slayer Helmet (with 60 Crafting).Smoking Kills is required to craft fused full slayer helmets and their variants.

Players who purchase this ability can add up to five fully charged rings of slaying (40 teleports) and up to six fully charged ferocious rings (30 teleports) to any full slayer helmet variant to gain access to their teleports. The number of rings a player can fuse to it depends on the tier of the helmet.

Players can recharge the teleports by fusing more rings, or by paying 5 co-op Slayer points. The helm does not gain the ring of life property of the ferocious ring, and already has the non-teleport properties of a ring of slaying. Fused slayer helmets benefit from the ferocious ring damage boost.
Learn new technique to attack ice strykewyrms Unique2,000Learn an ancient technique to attack ice strykewyrms without a fiery item (with 93 Slayer).This ability can replace a fire cape as a requirement to be assigned ice strykewyrms assignment. The player must have the required Slayer level, boosts will not work.
Learn how to attach the bonecrusher to your tool belt Unique500Learn how to attach the bonecrusher to your tool belt.Requires the bonecrusher from the shops.
Learn how to attach herbicide to your tool belt Unique500Learn how to attach herbicide to your tool belt.Requires the herbicide from the shops.
Learn how to attach seedicide to your tool belt Unique500Learn how to attach seedicide to your tool belt.Requires the seedicide from the shops.
Learn how to attach the charming imp on your tool belt Unique500Learn how to attach the charming imp to your tool belt.Requires the charming imp from the shops.
Attach the gold accumulator on your tool belt Unique500Learn how to attach the advanced gold accumulator to your tool belt.Requires the advanced gold accumulator from the shops.
Learn to move the souls in your personal slayer dungeon Unique1,000Learn how to control the souls in your slayer dungeon to move them between chambers.Allows the use of the Slayer master's Ouija board in the Sunken Pyramid.

Assignment

[edit source]

Block/prefer lists are shared among all Slayer masters, even when one does not assign the task block/preferred from another master.

ItemPrice
sold at		Notes
Cancel task 30Skips the player's current assignment without penalties. The next assignment will gain the player normal task progression and Slayer points. However, if a current task is cancelled by accepting a new task from Turael or Spria, task streak will reset and the player will need to complete four assignments before they can start earning points again. This is only worth buying if tasks are from Chaeldar, Sumona, Duradel, Kuradal, Morvran or Laniakea, unless you are near a 10th or 50th task. It would take a minimum of two tasks from Laniakea, Duradel or Kuradal and three from Chaeldar or Sumona to regain the points used in buying this service.
Block task 100Skips the player's current assignment without penalties and permanently removes the assignment from the player's task list - the player will not be assigned that monster again. There is a current maximum limit of 8 types of assignments that can be cancelled - one for every 50 quest points, up to a maximum of 400 quest points. Cancelling the removal of monsters allows the monster to be assigned again, but does not refund points spent removing the monster. Blocking a cluster task from Laniakea does not prevent the individual subset task from being rolled, for example blocking Creatures of Daemonheim can still roll Kal'gerion demon or Stalker creature tasks.
Extend task 30Extends the player's number of monsters to kill on that task by 20% (e.g., 180 Nihil would be 216 instead). While this can be used at any point during the task, the 20% is still based on the initial number of monsters. It does not provide extra slayer points, compared to boss slayer. You cannot extend a task that is the same as your previous task, if the previous task was extended.
Prefer task 100Extends the player's current assignment automatically and approximately doubles the chance for the assignment to appear as a task in the future.[1] There is a current maximum limit of 8 types of assignments that can be preferred - one for every 50 quest points, up to a maximum of 400 quest points. Cancelling a preferred task does not refund points spent preferring the monster.

Achievements

[edit | edit source]
  • Ferocious Upgrade ( 20) – Unlock the upgrade to the ferocious ring so it can be used in Morvran's arena.
    100 Slayer points required to unlock this upgrade.
  • Special Slayer Delivery ( 30) – Unlock the ability to craft the listed Slayer items.
    1,900 Slayer points required to learn several abilities from slayer masters.
  • Get Tooled Up ( 20) – Fill up your tool belt.
    2,500 Slayer points required to learn how to attach the bonecrusher, charming imp, herbicide, seedicide, and advanced gold accumulator to the tool belt.
  • Here to Slay ( 20) – Equip a Corrupted Slayer Helmet, upgraded with an ensouled spectral lens.
    2,500 Slayer points required to make a corrupted slayer helmet for the first time.
  • Finished unpacking! ( 15) – Access the fourth room of the player-owned slayer dungeon.
    3,000 Slayer points required to unlock the fourth room in the Sunken Pyramid.
  • Tirannwn achievements
    • Hard - Can I Have Some Morvran? ( 15) – Complete a Morvran slayer assignment that has been both preferred and extended.
      100 Slayer points required to prefer a task.

Update history

[edit | edit source]
This information has been compiled as part of the update history project. Some updates may not be included – see here for how to help out!
  • patch 23 March 2026 (Update):
    • Fixed an issue where players would lose slayer points if they were at the max points cap and completed a reaper task.
  • hotfix 23 March 2026 (Update):
    • Fixed an issue where the player would receive additional slayer points instead of the expected points if they had a Raptor slayer task active.
  • patch 3 April 2023 (Update):
    • Updated Mandrith's Slayer Points to align with Laniakea.
  • patch 26 July 2021 (Update):
    • The correct amount of Slayer points will now be awarded upon Task completion.
  • ninja 26 October 2020 (Update):
    • Slayer Masters will now teach the ability to use the Slayer Master's Ouija Board for 1,000 Slayer Points.
      • Using the Slayer Master's Ouija Board within the Sunken Pyramid will allow you to customise the placement of creatures in your Personal Slayer Dungeon.
  • patch 4 May 2020 (Update):
    • The chat message for receiving Slayer points now shows how many you have in total.
    • A new Slayer point icon has been created so that it can be displayed in the Reward interface.
  • patch 24 February 2020 (Update):
    • RuneScape Mobile: Improved the visibility of messages generated for users when interacting with the Slayer Rewards shop on mobile.
  • patch 16 September 2019 (Update):
    • Right-clicking on the prefer list within the slayer rewards interface will now show 'Remove preferences' rather than 'Allow this task'.
  • patch 19 August 2019 (Update):
    • Corrected the full slayer helmet tier 4 defence requirement in the slayer rewards shop to match its level.
  • ninja 20 August 2018 (Update):
    • Blocking/Preferring tasks now immediately adds them to the list on the interface.
  • ninja 13 November 2017 (Update):
    • The slayer reward shop has been graphically updated to be more in line with current interface standards.
  • patch 10 July 2017 (Update):
    • The 'prefer task' option on the slayer master reward interface will now appear disabled when the player does not have enough points.
  • patch 20 March 2017 (Update):
    • Players will no longer lose slayer points if they click out of the task selection interface after purchasing an additional Soul Reaper task.
  • patch 11 January 2016 (Update):
    • Familiars no longer block the 'deliver finishing blows quicker' slayer ability.
  • ninja 22 July 2014 (Update):
    • Players can now use Quick Chat to display their Slayer points.
  • patch 24 July 2012 (Update):
    • It is now possible to once again purchase the "Enable Aquanites" reward from the Slayer rewards interface with less than 100 points.
  • patch 7 February 2012 (Update):
    • Bank PIN protection has been added to the Slayer rewards interface.
  • patch 6 December 2011 (Update):
    • The tooltip on the Slayer rewards interface for "Never assign X again" has been shortened.
  • patch 3 March 2010 (Update):
    • The ability to deal killing blows faster to specific Slayer creatures can now be purchased
    • The ability to be assigned ice strykewyrms and aquanites from Kuradal can now be purchased.
    • A 5th task can now be blocked if the player has more than 250 quest points.
  • update 5 June 2008 (Update):
    • Added to game.

Trivia

[edit | edit source]
  • Originally, Smoking Kills was required to obtain points. This was removed in January 2014 as part of a rework of the Slayer skill. Some confusion arose, causing Jagex to clarify that the quest would be required to obtain full points.[2]
  • Slayer points were mistakenly added to the Grand Exchange with the update to the Raptor's chest of slaying on 4 May 2020 due to their dummy item having a new icon. The slayer points' starting price was 1 and had a buy limit of 100. When slayer points were removed from the Grand Exchange a week later, any remaining offers stated that the buy limit was -1.
  • If a player has all the requirements for Smoking Kills but haven't completed it, they are prompted to do so every time they complete a Slayer task with the message: "Complete the 'Smoking Kills' quest to be receive[sic] double points for each Slayer task completed."

References

If either task is on your prefer list, that's what you get (bias towards first roll)... Once normal and prefer roll is decided, [if] you have a VIP ticket, you get the choice between that roll and a third roll."
  2. ^ Mod Ryan. Save Slayer and Smoking Kills!. Future Game Updates Forums. 31 December 2013. (Archived from the original on 5 January 2014.)

properties.name: description
properties.*: Slayer points are a currency obtained by completing Slayer assignments or a special challenge from any Slayer Master except for Turael and Spria. The number rewarded depends on the Slayer Master, with the tougher masters awarding more points. Completion of the quest Smoking Kills will double the value...
